violations.claim: VIOLATION: Comment promises "one assert is (= A B), the other is (not (= A B))"
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P5
; LINE: 112
; VIOLATION: Comment promises "one assert is (= A B), the other is (not (= A B))"
; — implying the SAME A and B in both patterns. But the code allows different
; variable pairs. When variables differ, both asserts are simultaneously satisfiable,
; meaning the block is NOT tautologically unsat — yet fn returns true.

(declare-const val_eq_lhs Int)   ; semantic value: LHS variable in equality assert
(declare-const val_eq_rhs Int)   ; semantic value: RHS variable in equality assert
(declare-const val_neg_lhs Int)  ; semantic value: LHS variable in negation assert
(declare-const val_neg_rhs Int)  ; semantic value: RHS variable in negation assert

; Code transition: (= A B) assert forces val_eq_lhs = val_eq_rhs
(assert (= val_eq_lhs val_eq_rhs))

; Code transition: (not (= C D)) assert forces val_neg_lhs != val_neg_rhs
; C,D are DIFFERENT variables from A,B — the regex allows this
(assert (not (= val_neg_lhs val_neg_rhs)))

; Violation: both constraints are simultaneously satisfiable — block is SAT not UNSAT.
; isTrivialIdentity labels this block "trivial" but Z3 would return sat.
; Witness: val_eq_lhs=5, val_eq_rhs=5, val_neg_lhs=3, val_neg_rhs=7

(check-sat)

(check-sat)
